items: The prior day's rebound had been narrow and dependent on AI leadership.
U.S.-Iran tensions and the Strait of Hormuz remained active risks.
Oil supply policy and semiconductor valuation concerns were the main known cross-asset threats.

intraday_and_postclose_catalysts: The U.S. Treasury revoked an Iranian-oil sales waiver, increasing supply and shipping risk.
WTI crude surged, Treasury yields rose on inflation concerns, and the dollar firmed.
Chipmakers fell sharply; rotation into other sectors limited the S&P 500 decline.
causal_narrative: The market absorbed a direct inflation/geopolitical shock. Higher oil and yields compressed growth multiples and intensified semiconductor selling. The S&P 500 held up better than tech-heavy indexes because capital rotated rather than exiting equities uniformly.
alternative_explanations: Options and dealer positioning may amplify or oppose the public-news impulse.
Index concentration can make SPY and SPX diverge from equal-weight breadth.
